name: pyfirmata

metadata.name: pyFirmata
metadata.summary: A Python interface for the Firmata procotol

documentation:
# pyFirmata

pyFirmata is a Python interface for the [Firmata] protocol. It is fully
compatible with Firmata 2.1, and has some functionality of version 2.2. It runs
on Python 2.7, 3.3 and 3.4.

Test & coverage status:

```{image} https://travis-ci.org/tino/pyFirmata.png?branch=master
:target: https://travis-ci.org/tino/pyFirmata
```

```{image} https://coveralls.io/repos/github/tino/pyFirmata/badge.svg?branch=master
:target: https://coveralls.io/github/tino/pyFirmata?branch=master
```

## Installation

The preferred way to install is with [pip]:

```
pip install pyfirmata
```

You can also install from source with `python setup.py install`. You will
need to have [setuptools] installed:

```
git clone https://github.com/tino/pyFirmata
cd pyFirmata
python setup.py install
```

## Usage

Basic usage:

```
>>> from pyfirmata import Arduino, util
>>> board = Arduino('/dev/tty.usbserial-A6008rIF')
>>> board.digital[13].write(1)
```

To use analog ports, it is probably handy to start an iterator thread.
Otherwise the board will keep sending data to your serial, until it overflows:

```
>>> it = util.Iterator(board)
>>> it.start()
>>> board.analog[0].enable_reporting()
>>> board.analog[0].read()
0.661440304938
```

If you use a pin more often, it can be worth it to use the `get_pin` method
of the board. It let's you specify what pin you need by a string, composed of
'a' or 'd' (depending on wether you need an analog or digital pin), the pin
number, and the mode ('i' for input, 'o' for output, 'p' for pwm). All
seperated by `:`. Eg. `a:0:i` for analog 0 as input or `d:3:p` for
digital pin 3 as pwm.:

```
>>> analog_0 = board.get_pin('a:0:i')
>>> analog_0.read()
0.661440304938
>>> pin3 = board.get_pin('d:3:p')
>>> pin3.write(0.6)
```

## Board layout

If you want to use a board with a different layout than the standard Arduino
or the Arduino Mega (for which there exist the shortcut classes
`pyfirmata.Arduino` and `pyfirmata.ArduinoMega`), instantiate the Board
class with a dictionary as the `layout` argument. This is the layout dict
for the Mega for example:

```
>>> mega = {
...         'digital' : tuple(x for x in range(54)),
...         'analog' : tuple(x for x in range(16)),
...         'pwm' : tuple(x for x in range(2,14)),
...         'use_ports' : True,
...         'disabled' : (0, 1, 14, 15) # Rx, Tx, Crystal
...         }
```

## Todo

The next things on my list are to implement the new protocol changes in
firmata:

- Pin State Query, which allows it to populate on-screen controls with an
  accurate representation of the hardware's configuration
  (<http://firmata.org/wiki/Proposals#Pin_State_Query_.28added_in_version_2.2.29>)

[firmata]: http://firmata.org
[pip]: http://www.pip-installer.org/en/latest/
[setuptools]: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/setuptools

functions.name: pyfirmata.util.get_the_board
documentation:
Helper function to get the one and only board connected to the computer
running this. It assumes a normal arduino layout, but this can be
overriden by passing a different layout dict as the `layout` parameter.
`base_dir` and `identifier` are overridable as well. It will raise an
IOError if it can't find a board, on a serial, or if it finds more than
one.

functions.name: pyfirmata.util.to_two_bytes
documentation: Breaks an integer into two 7 bit bytes.

functions.name: pyfirmata.util.from_two_bytes
documentation: Return an integer from two 7 bit bytes.

functions.name: pyfirmata.util.two_byte_iter_to_str
documentation: Return a string made from a list of two byte chars.

functions.name: pyfirmata.util.str_to_two_byte_iter
documentation: Return a iter consisting of two byte chars from a string.

functions.name: pyfirmata.util.break_to_bytes
documentation:
Breaks a value into values of less than 255 that form value when multiplied.
(Or almost do so with primes)
Returns a tuple

functions.name: pyfirmata.util.pin_list_to_board_dict
documentation:
Capability Response codes:
    INPUT:  0, 1
    OUTPUT: 1, 1
    ANALOG: 2, 10
    PWM:    3, 8
    SERV0:  4, 14
    I2C:    6, 1

variables.name: pyfirmata.__version__
documentation: Use bumpversion!

documentation: A board that will set itself up as a normal Arduino.
shortcut classes

classes.name: pyfirmata.mockup.MockupSerial

methods.name: pyfirmata.mockup.MockupSerial.write
documentation: Appends bytes flat to the deque. So iterables will be unpacked.

A Mockup object for python's Serial. Functions as a fifo-stack. Push to
it with `write`, read from it with `read`.

variables.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.DIGITAL_MESSAGE
documentation: send data for a digital pin

variables.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.ANALOG_MESSAGE
documentation: send data for an analog pin (or PWM)

variables.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.DIGITAL_PULSE
documentation: SysEx command to send a digital pulse

variables.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.REPORT_ANALOG
documentation: enable analog input by pin #

variables.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.REPORT_DIGITAL
documentation: enable digital input by port pair

variables.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.START_SYSEX
documentation: start a MIDI SysEx msg

variables.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.SET_PIN_MODE
documentation: set a pin to INPUT/OUTPUT/PWM/etc

variables.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.END_SYSEX
documentation: end a MIDI SysEx msg

variables.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.REPORT_VERSION
documentation: report firmware version

variables.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.SYSTEM_RESET
documentation: reset from MIDI

variables.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.QUERY_FIRMWARE
documentation: query the firmware name

variables.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.EXTENDED_ANALOG
documentation: analog write (PWM, Servo, etc) to any pin

variables.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.PIN_STATE_QUERY
documentation: ask for a pin's current mode and value

variables.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.PIN_STATE_RESPONSE
documentation: reply with pin's current mode and value

variables.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.CAPABILITY_QUERY
documentation: ask for supported modes and resolution of all pins

variables.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.CAPABILITY_RESPONSE
documentation: reply with supported modes and resolution

variables.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.ANALOG_MAPPING_QUERY
documentation: ask for mapping of analog to pin numbers

variables.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.ANALOG_MAPPING_RESPONSE
documentation: reply with mapping info

variables.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.SERVO_CONFIG
documentation: set max angle, minPulse, maxPulse, freq

variables.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.STRING_DATA
documentation: a string message with 14-bits per char

variables.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.SHIFT_DATA
documentation: a bitstream to/from a shift register

variables.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.I2C_REQUEST
documentation: send an I2C read/write request

variables.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.I2C_REPLY
documentation: a reply to an I2C read request

variables.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.I2C_CONFIG
documentation: config I2C settings such as delay times and power pins

variables.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.REPORT_FIRMWARE
documentation: report name and version of the firmware

variables.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.SAMPLING_INTERVAL
documentation: set the poll rate of the main loop

variables.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.SYSEX_NON_REALTIME
documentation: MIDI Reserved for non-realtime messages

variables.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.SYSEX_REALTIME
documentation: MIDI Reserved for realtime messages

variables.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.INPUT
documentation: as defined in wiring.h

variables.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.OUTPUT
documentation: as defined in wiring.h

variables.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.ANALOG
documentation: analog pin in analogInput mode

variables.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.PWM
documentation: digital pin in PWM output mode

variables.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.SERVO
documentation: digital pin in SERVO mode

variables.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.DIGITAL
documentation: same as OUTPUT below

classes.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.Board

methods.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.Board.__del__
documentation:
The connection with the a board can get messed up when a script is
closed without calling board.exit() (which closes the serial
connection). Therefore also do it here and hope it helps.

methods.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.Board.setup_layout
documentation: Setup the Pin instances based on the given board layout.

methods.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.Board.auto_setup
documentation: Automatic setup based on Firmata's "Capability Query"

methods.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.Board.add_cmd_handler
documentation: Adds a command handler for a command.

methods.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.Board.get_pin
documentation:
Returns the activated pin given by the pin definition.
May raise an `InvalidPinDefError` or a `PinAlreadyTakenError`.

```
arg pin_def

Pin definition as described below,
but without the arduino name. So for example a:1:i.
```

'a' analog pin Pin number 'i' for input
'd' digital pin Pin number 'o' for output

> 'p' for pwm (Pulse-width modulation)

All seperated by `:`.

methods.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.Board.pass_time
documentation: Non-blocking time-out for `t` seconds.

methods.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.Board.send_sysex
documentation:
Sends a SysEx msg.

:arg sysex_cmd: A sysex command byte
: arg data: a bytearray of 7-bit bytes of arbitrary data

methods.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.Board.iterate
documentation:
Reads and handles data from the microcontroller over the serial port.
This method should be called in a main loop or in an {class}`Iterator`
instance to keep this boards pin values up to date.

methods.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.Board.get_firmata_version
documentation:
Returns a version tuple (major, minor) for the firmata firmware on the
board.

methods.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.Board.servo_config
documentation:
Configure a pin as servo with min_pulse, max_pulse and first angle.
`min_pulse` and `max_pulse` default to the arduino defaults.

methods.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.Board.exit
documentation: Call this to exit cleanly.

methods.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.Board._handle_analog_message
documentation: Command handlers

methods.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.Board._handle_digital_message
documentation:
Digital messages always go by the whole port. This means we have a
bitmask which we update the port.
The Base class for any board.

classes.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.Port

methods.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.Port.enable_reporting
documentation: Enable reporting of values for the whole port.

methods.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.Port.disable_reporting
documentation: Disable the reporting of the port.

methods.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.Port.write
documentation: Set the output pins of the port to the correct state.

methods.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.Port._update
documentation: Update the values for the pins marked as input with the mask.
An 8-bit port on the board.

classes.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.Pin

methods.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.Pin.enable_reporting
documentation: Set an input pin to report values.

methods.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.Pin.disable_reporting
documentation: Disable the reporting of an input pin.

methods.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.Pin.read
documentation:
Returns the output value of the pin. This value is updated by the
boards {meth}`Board.iterate` method. Value is always in the range from
0.0 to 1.0.

methods.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.Pin.write
documentation:
Output a voltage from the pin

:arg value: Uses value as a boolean if the pin is in output mode, or
    expects a float from 0 to 1 if the pin is in PWM mode. If the pin
    is in SERVO the value should be in degrees.

class_variables.name: pyfirmata.pyfirmata.Pin.mode
documentation:
Mode of operation for the pin. Can be one of the pin modes: INPUT, OUTPUT,
ANALOG, PWM. or SERVO (or UNAVAILABLE).
A Pin representation
